metadata.title: Who's Gonna Bell That Cat

cast.mice: use 2+ when mice are in a scene

metadata.compositing: each storyboard frame is a FLUX composite of beat.assets (<=4 inputs: background + characters); start_prompt -> stills/story/<beat>_start.png, end_prompt -> _end.png. Kling tweens start->end.
metadata.style: Composite the inputs into ONE cohesive hand-drawn ink-and-gouache storybook illustration — unified moonlit lighting, matching linework and texture, the characters blended naturally into the scene at consistent scale. No text, no watermark.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.lyric_text: Late one night behind the wall
beats.start_prompt: three little mice creep out along the base of the moonlit fence at night. Composite the inputs into ONE cohesive hand-drawn ink-and-gouache storybook illustration — unified moonlit lighting, matching linework and texture, the characters blended naturally into the scene at consistent scale. No text, no watermark.

Composite the inputs into ONE cohesive hand-drawn ink-and-gouache storybook illustration — unified moonlit lighting, matching linework and texture, the characters blended naturally into the scene at consistent scale. No text, no watermark.
